items.title: La casa donde nació OpenAI, creadora de ChatGPT, está en venta y esto es lo que cuesta
items.author: Pool CEO
items.summary:
OpenAI, dirigida por Sam Altman, fue creada en una casa en California, la cual está valuada en más de 1 millón de dólares. 
La publicación La casa donde nació OpenAI, creadora de ChatGPT, está en venta y esto es lo que cuesta apareció primero en EL CEO.
verbatimFacts: En 2015 , Sam Altman y Greg Brockman iniciaron un proyecto que cambiaría para siempre a la industria tecnológica: la creación de OpenAI .
El nacimiento de la empresa ocurrió en un lugar poco convencional: una casa ubicada en el barrio de Mission , en San Francisco, California .

Shane Ray , agente inmobiliario de Compass , comentó en una entrevista para La Nación que en esa propiedad, construida en 1885 , se pensaron y desarrollaron los conceptos base de OpenAI, aunque no se conoce con precisión la fecha en la que dejaron de trabajar ahí.
Además, el inmueble apareció en la película Artificial , que narra la historia del despido y posterior regreso de Sam Altman a OpenAI.
Actualmente, la casa, de 170 metros cuadrados , se encuentra a la venta por 1.545 millones de dólares .
Sin embargo, Greg Brockman, quien fue el dueño original, no ha dado más detalles sobre la comercialización del inmueble.
No solo OpenAI: más tecnológicas nacieron en casas La idea de que una de las empresas más importantes del mundo nazca en la sala de una casa o en el cuarto de un universitario puede sonar a película.
items.rawContent:
En 2015, Sam Altman y Greg Brockman iniciaron un proyecto que cambiaría para siempre a la industria tecnológica: la creación de OpenAI. El nacimiento de la empresa ocurrió en un lugar poco convencional: una casa ubicada en el barrio de Mission, en San Francisco, California.

Shane Ray, agente inmobiliario de Compass, comentó en una entrevista para La Nación que en esa propiedad, construida en 1885, se pensaron y desarrollaron los conceptos base de OpenAI, aunque no se conoce con precisión la fecha en la que dejaron de trabajar ahí.

Además, el inmueble apareció en la película Artificial, que narra la historia del despido y posterior regreso de Sam Altman a OpenAI.

Actualmente, la casa, de 170 metros cuadrados, se encuentra a la venta por 1.545 millones de dólares. Sin embargo, Greg Brockman, quien fue el dueño original, no ha dado más detalles sobre la comercialización del inmueble.

No solo OpenAI: más tecnológicas nacieron en casas

La idea de que una de las empresas más importantes del mundo nazca en la sala de una casa o en el cuarto de un universitario puede sonar a película. Sin embargo, en la industria tecnológica se trata de un fenómeno recurrente.

En 2004, Mark Zuckerberg dio origen a Facebook, una de las redes sociales más influyentes del mundo, desde una habitación en la Universidad de Harvard, en Massachusetts. Un caso similar fue el de Google, que comenzó en una residencia vinculada a la Universidad de Stanford.

Otro ejemplo emblemático fue el de Steve Jobs y Steve Wozniak, quienes desarrollaron los primeros productos de Apple dentro de un garaje. Algo similar ocurri

items.title: Ariadna Montiel asume dirigencia nacional de Morena entre tensiones internas y presión externa
items.author: Enrique Legorreta
items.summary:
La nueva presidenta de Morena, Ariadna Montiel llega a la dirigencia del partido en medio de las acusaciones por parte de EU a miembros del movimiento
La publicación Ariadna Montiel asume dirigencia nacional de Morena entre tensiones internas y presión externa apareció primero en EL CEO.
verbatimFacts: Tal como se anticipaba, Ariadna Montiel , hasta hace unos días secretaria del Bienestar, fue electa por el Consejo General de Morena como presidenta del Comité Ejecutivo Nacional.
Su llegada ocurre en medio de tensiones internas y de señalamientos recientes desde Estados Unidos hacia integrantes del partido y gobernadores vinculados al llamado movimiento de transformación.

Desde las 11:00 horas, más de 1,800 congresistas del partido fundado por Andrés Manuel López Obrador se reunieron en el World Trade Center de la Ciudad de México , sede habitual de los encuentros del Consejo Nacional para llevar a cabo la Octava Sesión extraordinaria.
El encargado de formalizar la designación fue Alfonso Durazo , gobernador de Sonora y presidente del Consejo Nacional.
En su mensaje, llamó a cuidar la unidad interna del partido y advirtió que la forma en que se resuelvan las diferencias será observada por la ciudadanía.
La manera en que resolvamos nuestras diferencias será observada por el pueblo.
Ningún proyecto de transformación está exento de las tensiones propias de toda disputa por el poder político”, expresó.
items.rawContent:
Tal como se anticipaba, Ariadna Montiel, hasta hace unos días secretaria del Bienestar, fue electa por el Consejo General de Morena como presidenta del Comité Ejecutivo Nacional. Su llegada ocurre en medio de tensiones internas y de señalamientos recientes desde Estados Unidos hacia integrantes del partido y gobernadores vinculados al llamado movimiento de transformación.

Desde las 11:00 horas, más de 1,800 congresistas del partido fundado por Andrés Manuel López Obrador se reunieron en el World Trade Center de la Ciudad de México, sede habitual de los encuentros del Consejo Nacional para llevar a cabo la Octava Sesión extraordinaria.

El encargado de formalizar la designación fue Alfonso Durazo, gobernador de Sonora y presidente del Consejo Nacional. En su mensaje, llamó a cuidar la unidad interna del partido y advirtió que la forma en que se resuelvan las diferencias será observada por la ciudadanía.

La manera en que resolvamos nuestras diferencias será observada por el pueblo. Ningún proyecto de transformación está exento de las tensiones propias de toda disputa por el poder político”, expresó.

Ariadna Montiel llama a la unidad y defensa del proyecto

Tras rendir protesta como presidenta nacional de Morena, Ariadna Montiel afirmó que la prioridad del movimiento sigue siendo la defensa de los derechos del pueblo y la consolidación del proyecto político iniciado en años recientes.

Le hago un llamado a la militancia de Morena y al pueblo de México para mantenernos unidos y firmes en torno a este proyecto de nación. Nacimos en la resistencia y vamos a hacer frente a los embates del extranjero”, declaró.

Sobre el caso de Rubén Rocha Moya, Montiel retomó la postura oficial del partido al asegurar que Morena respalda la justicia, pero rechaza cualquier intento de intervención externa.

items.title: I sold my childhood home in Miami to buy an apartment in Sicily. We paid under $600,000 and did $98,000 in renovations.
items.author: Lauren Crosby Medlicott
items.summary:
Kat Moore and her husband bought an apartment in Sicily.
Courtesy of Kat Moore

Kat Moore is a 38-year-old teacher living in San Clemente, California.
Moore and her husband visited Sicily and decided to buy and renovate an apartment. 
Kat says the "adventure" has connected her to her mother, who moved from Cuba to start a new life.

This as-told-to essay is based on a conversation with Kat Moore. It has been edited for length and clarity.
In 2022, my husband and I visited Sicily for the first time. I fell in love with the island and told my husband I wanted to buy a house there to live in one
verbatimFacts: Kat Moore and her husband bought an apartment in Sicily.
Courtesy of Kat Moore Kat Moore is a 38-year-old teacher living in San Clemente, California.
Moore and her husband visited Sicily and decided to buy and renovate an apartment.
Kat says the "adventure" has connected her to her mother, who moved from Cuba to start a new life.
This as-told-to essay is based on a conversation with Kat Moore.
It has been edited for length and clarity.
In 2022, my husband and I visited Sicily for the first time.
items.rawContent:
• Kat Moore is a 38-year-old teacher living in San Clemente, California.
  • Moore and her husband visited Sicily and decided to buy and renovate an apartment.
  • Kat says the "adventure" has connected her to her mother, who moved from Cuba to start a new life.

This as-told-to essay is based on a conversation with Kat Moore. It has been edited for length and clarity.

In 2022, my husband and I visited Sicily for the first time. I fell in love with the island and told my husband I wanted to buy a house there to live in one day. He thought I was insane, but I was adamant.

People in Sicily tend to lead a slower pace of life. In the US, there's a huge emphasis on work as your identity. It's stressful and overwhelming, and I craved a different way of life. A different pace.

Three years later, in March 2025, we were in Sicily for spring break, and I viewed 50 houses to get an idea of what was on offer and how much we'd be spending.

When we saw one of the last places we viewed, an apartment in Ortigia, the historic heart of Sicily, with a balcony overlooking the sea, I just knew — this was the house we'd buy.

I was ready to sell the house I grew up in

I wanted to put in an offer right away, but my husband questioned whether it was a financially sound move. I told him I was ready to sell my mom's house in Miami — the family home I'd grown up in.

I was connected to my mom's house, having held onto it even though my mom had died 14 years prior. She was Cuban and moved to the US during political unrest in Cuba in the 1950s and 1960s. She risked everything to find a better life in the United States, uprooting herself and starting fresh in a place and culture she didn't know.

items.title: Argentines Keep Cash Under Mattresses Even as Milei Pushes Bank Accounts
items.author: David Feliba
items.summary: You’d be surprised how damp and smelly dollar bills get, Alejandro Lamas says, when they’ve been squirreled away under a mattress for years. He’s gotten so many stacks of them from Argentines he’s sold used cars to over the years that he’s a bit of a connoisseur. Slip him a counterfeit and he knows at first touch.
verbatimFacts: You’d be surprised how damp and smelly dollar bills get, Alejandro Lamas says, when they’ve been squirreled away under a mattress for years.
He’s gotten so many stacks of them from Argentines he’s sold used cars to over the years that he’s a bit of a connoisseur.
Slip him a counterfeit and he knows at first touch.

items.title: I run a business while traveling the world. I couldn't have afforded to scale it while living in the US.
items.author: Jordan Pandy
items.summary:
Ryan Garrido runs a business and travels the world as a digital nomad.
Courtesy of Ryan Garrido

Ryan Garrido runs a dropshipping business while living abroad to reduce costs.
His savings on daily expenses helped him get his business off the ground.
He said it's worth it, but accepts that there are tradeoffs to living abroad as a business owner.

This as-told-to essay is based on a conversation with Ryan Garrido, the owner of Dropshipping High Ticket, a dropshipping business that he runs while traveling the world. Garrido, 37, started his company while living overseas. The following has been e
verbatimFacts: Ryan Garrido runs a business and travels the world as a digital nomad.
Courtesy of Ryan Garrido Ryan Garrido runs a dropshipping business while living abroad to reduce costs.
His savings on daily expenses helped him get his business off the ground.
He said it's worth it, but accepts that there are tradeoffs to living abroad as a business owner.
This as-told-to essay is based on a conversation with Ryan Garrido, the owner of Dropshipping High Ticket, a dropshipping business that he runs while traveling the world.
Garrido, 37, started his company while living overseas.
The following has been edited for length and clarity.
items.rawContent:
• Ryan Garrido runs a dropshipping business while living abroad to reduce costs.
  • His savings on daily expenses helped him get his business off the ground.
  • He said it's worth it, but accepts that there are tradeoffs to living abroad as a business owner.

This as-told-to essay is based on a conversation with Ryan Garrido, the owner of Dropshipping High Ticket, a dropshipping business that he runs while traveling the world. Garrido, 37, started his company while living overseas. The following has been edited for length and clarity.

I think the definition of an entrepreneur is someone who takes risks. In America, you might be thinking, "OK, I have enough to break even. I can pay my rent, I can pay my bills, I can pay for groceries, my gas, and I'll have $20 left over."

But what business are you going to do with $20?

When you have excess funds, you can take risks, and I think that's the common issue with people living in America. There's no room to take risks because there are no additional funds.

Had I tried to have a business in the States, the cost of living might have kept me from taking the leap. So the fact that I was able to not have my daily costs be such a burden — my cost of living is 10% or 20% of my income at this point in Thailand, it's very minimal — allows me to fully invest in the company, to scale up more advertising, and just to do it in the first place.

You also broaden your horizon dramatically when living abroad because you start to see other cultures and how they do things versus how we do things.

I got into dropshipping while traveling the world

Before I moved abroad, I had a sales job in the US — I was doing B2B sales and door-to-door sales in DC. I was recruiting and had a team of guys that I was managing. Then I discovered meditation and wanted to study it full-time, so I left the country to go study full-time in South Korea.

A friend of mine was traveling all over the world, doing speaking events and posting them on Facebook. I messaged him one

items.title: 5 big ideas shaping journalism’s next chapter
items.author: Jeremy Caplan
items.summary:
This article is republished with permission from Wonder Tools, a newsletter that helps you discover the most useful sites and apps.

I spoke on a panel about how journalism training evolves when AI does entry-level work. I also attended 15 other sessions. Five ideas stuck with me, each about how journalism can be more human, more sustainable, and more inventive, eve
verbatimFacts: This article is republished with permission from Wonder Tools , a newsletter that helps you discover the most useful sites and apps.
The recent International Journalism Festival in Perugia, Italy drew 2,000+ journalists, including 526 speakers , for four days of conversation about what’s next for our field.
It was one of the most vibrant conferences I’ve attended.
I spoke on a panel about how journalism training evolves when AI does entry-level work.

Live Journalism Resonates Madrid-based Diario Vivo puts journalists and ordinary people on stage to tell personal stories.
Audiences don’t know what stories they’ll hear when they arrive at a show.
items.rawContent:
This article is republished with permission from Wonder Tools, a newsletter that helps you discover the most useful sites and apps.

The recent International Journalism Festival in Perugia, Italy drew 2,000+ journalists, including 526 speakers, for four days of conversation about what’s next for our field. It was one of the most vibrant conferences I’ve attended.

I spoke on a panel about how journalism training evolves when AI does entry-level work. I also attended 15 other sessions. Five ideas stuck with me, each about how journalism can be more human, more sustainable, and more inventive, even as the industry contracts.

Live Journalism Resonates

Madrid-based Diario Vivo puts journalists and ordinary people on stage to tell personal stories. Nothing is recorded. It’s pure human storytelling.

Audiences don’t know what stories they’ll hear when they arrive at a show. Founder Vanessa Rousselot says the format is designed to make people laugh and cry and to restore trust between journalists and the public. Diario Vivo began in 2017 with 100 people in the audience. Now they sell out 1,000-seat venues. More than 25,000 people have seen their shows thus far, in various cities.

Correctiv, a German nonprofit newsroom, turns its investigations into theater performed by professional actors. Editor in chief Jean Peters says Correctiv is building a network of 50+ theaters across Europe to distribute journalistic productions. Correctiv’s publisher, David Schraven, estimated that each two-hour theater performance is “equivalent to 3.6 million seconds spent on TikTok, but with a much bigger impact.”

These projects follow in the tradition of Pop-Up Magazine, which launched in California in 2009 and hosted sold-out shows for tens of thousands of people around North America until closing in 2023 after the pandemic devastated their business. Diar

items.title: I took my 8-year-old to the Miami Grand Prix. We enjoyed all the events, but the heat was unbearable.
items.author: Conz Preti
items.summary:
The author took her son to the F1 races.
Courtesy of the author

My dad and my son bond over watching F1 races whenever they're together.
My dad invited my son to the Miami Grand Prix for his 8th birthday.
I prepared by getting noise-canceling headphones for everyone.

I always joke that my dad really wanted a son, but instead he had me, an only daughter. 
He made me a huge sports fan. Like him, I love soccer, and we also bonded over watching F1 races.
When my son was born, my dad was over the moon about having an eventual buddy for all his sports adventures. He got my son really into racing,
verbatimFacts: Courtesy of the author My dad and my son bond over watching F1 races whenever they're together.
My dad invited my son to the Miami Grand Prix for his 8th birthday.
I prepared by getting noise-canceling headphones for everyone.
I always joke that my dad really wanted a son, but instead he had me, an only daughter .
Like him, I love soccer, and we also bonded over watching F1 races.
When my son was born, my dad was over the moon about having an eventual buddy for all his sports adventures.
He got my son really into racing, and they watched the F1 together every Sunday.
items.rawContent:
• My dad and my son bond over watching F1 races whenever they're together.
  • My dad invited my son to the Miami Grand Prix for his 8th birthday.
  • I prepared by getting noise-canceling headphones for everyone.

I always joke that my dad really wanted a son, but instead he had me, an only daughter.

He made me a huge sports fan. Like him, I love soccer, and we also bonded over watching F1 races.

When my son was born, my dad was over the moon about having an eventual buddy for all his sports adventures. He got my son really into racing, and they watched the F1 together every Sunday.

When my son turned eight, he asked to go to a race with my dad and me. We took him this weekend to the F1 race in Miami, and it was an unforgettable experience, for many reasons.

Getting to the race was straightforward

I was nervous about the logistics of arriving at the circuit in Miami. It was actually pretty straightforward, and we made it right as the parking lots opened at 9:30 a.m.

Getting in was also easy because we were so early. Doors opened at 10 a.m., and we headed straight to our seats to watch the F2 race to warm up for the excitement.

My son wanted some merch as a souvenir, but all the hats were $100 or more, so we skipped — I had purchased last year's caps from eBay for $15 prior the race. One that was designed especially for the race sold out before the end of the day.

Prices were high, but not as much as in other sports

I recently went to see Inter Miami, and I was shocked by the prices of food and beer. At the race, p

items.title: ¿Cómo almacena y qué tratamiento hace la IA con la información personal?
items.author: Gerardo Farell
items.summary:
Cada motor de IA guarda de manera distinta la información que sus usuarios generan diariamente.
La publicación ¿Cómo almacena y qué tratamiento hace la IA con la información personal? apareció primero en EL CEO.
verbatimFacts: El uso de herramientas de Inteligencia Artificial es cada vez más frecuente para solucionar actividades , responder preguntas y programar o escribir correos, lo que vuelve la IA como una infraestructura digital básica.

En realidad, interactuar con grandes modelos de lenguaje implica una compleja red de almacenamiento y procesamiento de datos que puede exponer secretos comerciales o información personal sensible si no se tiene cuidado”, comenta Mario Micucci, investigador de seguridad informática de ESET Latinoamérica.

Cada motor de IA guarda de manera distinta la información que sus usuarios generan, sus peticiones, pero en el caso de ChatGPT, ese contenido no solo se guarda en el navegador, sino recorre un camino.

Foto: Nubosoft Las tres capas de ChatGPT Según la empresa de seguridad informática ESET, en el caso del programa desarrollado por la empresa OpenAI guarda información en tres capas o sitios que consideran fundamentales para el tratamiento de datos.
Uno es el almacenamiento en los servidores de la empresa tecnológica para acceder a ese historial en otros dispositivos y se conserva por periodos limitados
items.rawContent:
El uso de herramientas de Inteligencia Artificial es cada vez más frecuente para solucionar actividades, responder preguntas y programar o escribir correos, lo que vuelve la IA como una infraestructura digital básica.

De acuerdo con datos de OpenAI, existen al menos 900 millones de usuarios activos de ChatGPT, no obstante, no existe certeza del uso que se dan a los datos que se almacenan y a las respuestas que da a los usuarios, incluso en 2024, más de 225,000 registros de inicio de sesión en la app de IA los encontraron en foros de la Dark Web, credenciales robadas mediante el uso del malware tipo infostealer.

“Muchas personas pueden creer que las conversaciones son volátiles y funcionan como un chat privado que desaparece al cerrar la ventana. En realidad, interactuar con grandes modelos de lenguaje implica una compleja red de almacenamiento y procesamiento de datos que puede exponer secretos comerciales o información personal sensible si no se tiene cuidado”, comenta Mario Micucci, investigador de seguridad informática de ESET Latinoamérica.

Cada motor de IA guarda de manera distinta la información que sus usuarios generan, sus peticiones, pero en el caso de ChatGPT, ese contenido no solo se guarda en el navegador, sino recorre un camino.

Las tres capas de ChatGPT

Según la empresa de seguridad informática ESET, en el caso del programa desarrollado por la empresa OpenAI guarda información en tres capas o sitios que consideran fundamentales para el tratamiento de datos.

Uno es el almacenamiento en los servidores de la empresa tecnológica para acceder a ese historial en otros dispositivos y se conserva por periodos limitados

items.title: US Navy Taps AI Firm to Clear Iranian Mines in Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Traffic through the waterway has been at a near-standstill since the US and Israel launched strikes against Iran in late February and Tehran threatened to attack ships in the strait. Iran has said it laid mines along the most frequently used routes of the narrow waterway, and a senior US Defense Department official says clearing the strait of mines could take six months. Domino Data Lab Chief Operating Officer Thomas Robinson joins David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss the adoption of artificial intelligence (AI) to detect Iranian sea mines in the Strait of Horm
verbatimFacts: Traffic through the waterway has been at a near-standstill since the US and Israel launched strikes against Iran in late February and Tehran threatened to attack ships in the strait.
Iran has said it laid mines along the most frequently used routes of the narrow waterway, and a senior US Defense Department official says clearing the strait of mines could take six months.

Domino Data Lab Chief Operating Officer Thomas Robinson joins David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss the adoption of artificial intelligence (AI) to detect Iranian sea mines in the Strait of Hormuz to speed up and improve the accuracy of underwater mine detection while reducing reliance on human personnel. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Big Tech Earnings Show Split Between AI Trade Winners and Losers
items.author: Jeran Wittenstein, Ryan Vlastelica
items.summary: The world’s biggest technology companies posted strong earnings last week, showing that the artificial intelligence boom is alive and well. But in the stock market, investors are getting more granular as they try to divvy up the winners and losers in the AI trade.
verbatimFacts: The world’s biggest technology companies posted strong earnings last week, showing that the artificial intelligence boom is alive and well. But in the stock market, investors are getting more granular as they try to divvy up the winners and losers in the AI trade.

items.title: I flew to Berkshire Hathaway's first big bash without Warren Buffett as host. I found an event with an uncertain future.
items.author: Theron Mohamed
items.summary:
I snapped a picture in front of the welcome sign at Berkshire's meeting this year.
Theron Mohamed/Business Insider

I flew to Omaha for Berkshire Hathaway's first shareholder meeting with Greg Abel as CEO.
Several attendees cast doubt on the annual gathering's future without Warren Buffett hosting it.
Many said the meeting has lost some appeal now that Buffett and Charlie Munger are no longer onstage.

I flew to Omaha this week to attend the first Berkshire Hathaway shareholder meeting in six decades not led by Warren Buffett as CEO.
My conversations with more than 20 shareholders, analysts, a
verbatimFacts: I snapped a picture in front of the welcome sign at Berkshire's meeting this year.
Theron Mohamed/Business Insider I flew to Omaha for Berkshire Hathaway's first shareholder meeting with Greg Abel as CEO.
Several attendees cast doubt on the annual gathering's future without Warren Buffett hosting it.
Many said the meeting has lost some appeal now that Buffett and Charlie Munger are no longer onstage.
I flew to Omaha this week to attend the first Berkshire Hathaway shareholder meeting in six decades not led by Warren Buffett as CEO.
My conversations with more than 20 shareholders, analysts, and executives pointed to an uncertain future for the iconic gathering now that Buffett has stepped out of the spotlight.

Berkshire's annual bash has been dubbed "Woodstock for Capitalists" because it attracts tens of thousands of people from across the world to Buffett's hometown of Omaha for a weekend of shopping, learning, networking, and more .
items.rawContent:
• I flew to Omaha for Berkshire Hathaway's first shareholder meeting with Greg Abel as CEO.
  • Several attendees cast doubt on the annual gathering's future without Warren Buffett hosting it.
  • Many said the meeting has lost some appeal now that Buffett and Charlie Munger are no longer onstage.

I flew to Omaha this week to attend the first Berkshire Hathaway shareholder meeting in six decades not led by Warren Buffett as CEO.

My conversations with more than 20 shareholders, analysts, and executives pointed to an uncertain future for the iconic gathering now that Buffett has stepped out of the spotlight.

Berkshire's annual bash has been dubbed "Woodstock for Capitalists" because it attracts tens of thousands of people from across the world to Buffett's hometown of Omaha for a weekend of shopping, learning, networking, and more.

Buffett, 95, rocked the business world during last year's meeting when he unexpectedly announced he would retire at the end of the year. His hand-picked successor and the head of Berkshire's non-insurance operations, Greg Abel, took over as CEO on New Year's Day 2026.

Ajay Sharma, 42, a graduate research assistant at the University of Nebraska Medical Center, moonlights as a ride-share worker. While driving me to my hotel on Thursday night, he told me what it was like during last year's meeting.

"For a part-time Uber driver, it was like Christmas coming," he said in a follow-up email.

"I made double the hourly money I usually make," he continued. "I picked up people from hotels 12 miles away from the city center, as all the hotels were full, and there were no places in the restaurants."

Buffett's decision this year to sit in the audience during the weekend's main event, an hourslong Q&A session, and have Abel host it instead, led many to expect a smaller turnout this year.

Sharma said a prospective decline in attendance was a "real concern," as "every

items.title: Sununu: Spirit Bailout "Made No Financial Sense Whatsoever"
items.summary: The collapse of Spirit Airlines is cascading through airports across the US, leaving passengers stranded mid-journey and sending rival carriers into rapid-response mode to contain the damage. Airlines for America President & CEO Chris Sununu joins David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Oxxo avanza en su expansión; esta es la cantidad de tiendas que operan en 2026
items.author: Pool CEO
items.summary:
FEMSA inauguró durante el primer trimestre del año 158 sucursales de Oxxo en el país y se consolida como uno de sus negocios principales.
La publicación Oxxo avanza en su expansión; esta es la cantidad de tiendas que operan en 2026 apareció primero en EL CEO.
verbatimFacts: El negocio de proximidad en México de Fomento Económico Mexicano ( FEMSA ) continúa consolidándose como uno de los más dinámicos dentro del sector retail, y durante el primer trimestre del año reportó un nuevo avance en la expansión de su cadena Oxxo .

Oxxo y el número total de tiendas De acuerdo con su reporte financiero, durante el primer trimestre de 2026 (1T26) la compañía inauguró 158 nuevas tiendas Oxxo en distintos estados del país, por lo que se mantiene como uno de los negocios más relevantes del grupo.

Con ello, la base total de tiendas en operación alcanzó las 24,455 unidades al 31 de marzo de este año, lo que representa un crecimiento de 3.8% frente a las 23,567 tiendas registradas en el mismo periodo de 2025.

Este crecimiento ocurre en un entorno donde las tiendas de conveniencia han ganado mayor relevancia como puntos de consumo cotidiano, impulsadas por su cercanía, horarios extendidos y una oferta cada vez más diversificada de productos y servicios.
Fotoarte: Natalia Montiel El buen negocio de FEMSA La cadena Oxxo es considerada una de las “joyas de la corona” del conglomerado mexicano, y sus resultados financieros respaldan esa posición.
Los ingresos ascendieron a 74,424 millones de pesos <span style="font-

El negocio de proximidad en México de Fomento Económico Mexicano (FEMSA) continúa consolidándose como uno de los más dinámicos dentro del sector retail, y durante el primer trimestre del año reportó un nuevo avance en la expansión de su cadena Oxxo.

Oxxo y el número total de tiendas

De acuerdo con su reporte financiero, durante el primer trimestre de 2026 (1T26) la compañía inauguró 158 nuevas tiendas Oxxo en distintos estados del país, por lo que se mantiene como uno de los negocios más relevantes del grupo.

Con ello, la base total de tiendas en operación alcanzó las 24,455 unidades al 31 de marzo de este año, lo que representa un crecimiento de 3.8% frente a las 23,567 tiendas registradas en el mismo periodo de 2025.

Este crecimiento ocurre en un entorno donde las tiendas de conveniencia han ganado mayor relevancia como puntos de consumo cotidiano, impulsadas por su cercanía, horarios extendidos y una oferta cada vez más diversificada de productos y servicios.

items.title: Trillions in Retirement Dollars Flow Into Opaque Trusts

items.title: The Next Generation of Law Firm Deals
items.author: Carolyn Vallejo
items.summary:
The Law Practice Exchange's Tom Lenfesty is back on the Middle Market Growth podcast
The post The Next Generation of Law Firm Deals appeared first on ACG Insights (Formerly Middle Market Growth).
verbatimFacts: In the last fifteen months, private equity investment in law firms has gone from theoretical to reality.

Tom Lenfestey of the Law Practice Exchange is back with the podcast to talk about new legal and regulatory developments, what drives multiples in law firm valuations, the cultural elements dealmakers should consider, and more.
Preorder The Exit Blueprint: The Law Firm Owner’s Playbook for Selling, Succession, and Life After Law on Amazon .
This episode is brought to you by Hilco Global.

This year has brought some new developments for legal services M&A and Tom is back with us to discuss them.
